name: python-lokalise-api

metadata.name: python-lokalise-api
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Official Python interface for the Lokalise API v2

documentation:
# Lokalise API v2 official Python interface

![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/python-lokalise-api)
![CI](https://github.com/lokalise/python-lokalise-api/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/lokalise/python-lokalise-api/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://coveralls.io/github/lokalise/python-lokalise-api?branch=master)
[![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/python-lokalise-api)](https://pepy.tech/project/python-lokalise-api)
[![Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/python-lokalise-api/badge/?version=latest&style=flat)](https://python-lokalise-api.readthedocs.io)

Official Python 3 interface for the [Lokalise APIv2](https://developers.lokalise.com/reference/lokalise-rest-api) that represents returned data as Python objects.

## Quick start

This plugin requires Python 3.8 and above. Install it:

    pip install python-lokalise-api

Obtain a Lokalise API token (in your *Personal profile*) and use it:

```python
import lokalise
client = lokalise.Client('YOUR_API_TOKEN')
project = client.project('123.abc')
print(project.name)

client.upload_file(project.project_id, {
    "data": 'ZnI6DQogIHRlc3Q6IHRyYW5zbGF0aW9u',
    "filename": 'python_upload.yml',
    "lang_iso": 'en'
})

translation_keys = client.keys(project.project_id, {"page": 2,
    "limit": 3,
    "disable_references": "1"})
translation_keys.items[0].key_name['web'] # => "sign_up"
```

You can also use [OAuth 2 tokens](https://python-lokalise-api.readthedocs.io/en/latest/additional_info/oauth2_flow.html):

```python
client = lokalise.OAuthClient('YOUR_OAUTH2_API_TOKEN')

project = client.project('123.abc')
```

## Documentation

Find detailed documentation at [python-lokalise-api.readthedocs.io](https://python-lokalise-api.readthedocs.io).

## License

This plugin is licensed under the [BSD 3 Clause License](https://github.com/lokalise/python-lokalise-api/blob/master/LICENSE).

Copyright (c) [Lokalise group](https://lokalise.com) and [Ilya Krukowski](http://bodrovis.tech)

lokalise.models.language

Module containing language model.

classes.name: lokalise.models.language.LanguageModel
documentation: Describes project or system language.

modules.name: lokalise.errors
documentation:
lokalise.errors
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Defines custom exception classes.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.ClientError

methods.name: lokalise.errors.ClientError.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new exception.

```
param msg

Exception message

param code

Exception code (derived from HTTP status code)
```
General exception class.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.BadRequest
documentation:
The provided request is incorrect,
often due to missing a required parameter. HTTP status code 400.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.Unauthorized
documentation: API token is incorrect. HTTP status code 401.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.Forbidden
documentation:
The authenticated user does not have sufficient rights to perform the
desired action. HTTP status code 403.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.NotFound
documentation: The provided endpoint (resource) cannot be found. HTTP status code 404.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.MethodNowAllowed
documentation:
HTTP request with the provided verb is not supported by the endpoint.
HTTP status code 405.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.NotAcceptable
documentation: Posted resource is malformed. HTTP status code 406.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.Conflict
documentation: Request conflicts with another request. HTTP status code 409.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.Locked
documentation:
Your token is used simultaneously in multiple requests.
HTTP status code 423.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.TooManyRequests
documentation: Too many requests hit the API too quickly. HTTP status code 429.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.ServerError
documentation: Server-side error. HTTP status code 500.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.BadGateway
documentation: Server-side error. HTTP status code 502.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.ServiceUnavailable
documentation: Server is not available at the moment. HTTP status code 503.

classes.name: lokalise.errors.GatewayTimeout
documentation: Server has not responded in a timely fashion. HTTP status code 504.

modules.name: lokalise.models.base_model
documentation:
lokalise.models.base_model
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Model parent class inherited by specific models.

classes.name: lokalise.models.base_model.BaseModel

methods.name: lokalise.models.base_model.BaseModel.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new model.
A model describes a single resource, for example a project or a contributor.
To read raw data returned by the API, use the `raw_data` attribute.

```
param raw_data

Data returned by the API
```

methods.name: lokalise.models.base_model.BaseModel.__str__
documentation: Converts a model to string

methods.name: lokalise.models.base_model.BaseModel.__extract_common_attrs
documentation:
Fetches common data from the response and sets the
corresponding attributes. If the same attribute is present in model-specific
attribute list, it has higher priority.

Abstract base class for model objects.

:attribute ATTRS: list of attributes a resource contains. For example, a project
has a name, a description, and an ID.

:attribute COMMON_ATTRS: list of common attributes that the models may have.

:attribute DATA_KEY: contains the key name that should be used to fetch
data. Response usually arrives in the following format:
{"project_id": "abc", contributor: {"user_id": 1}}
In this case, the DATA_KEY would be "contributor"

modules.name: lokalise.utils
documentation:
lokalise.utils
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module contains various utility functions.

functions.name: lokalise.utils.snake_to_camel
documentation:
Converts string in snake case to camel case.
For example, "test_string" becomes "TestString"

functions.name: lokalise.utils.to_list
documentation:
Converts an object to a list. If the object is already a list,
does nothing.

```
param obj

Object to convert
```

modules.name: lokalise.client
documentation:
lokalise.client
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module contains API client definition.

classes.name: lokalise.client.Client

methods.name: lokalise.client.Client.reset_client
documentation: Resets the API client by clearing all attributes.

methods.name: lokalise.client.Client.get_endpoint
documentation:
Lazily loads an endpoint with a given name and stores it
under a specific instance attribute. For example, if the `name`
is "projects", then it will load .endpoints.projects_endpoint module
and then set attribute like this:
    self.__projects_endpoint = ProjectsEndpoint(self)

:param str name: Endpoint name to load
=== Endpoint helpers

methods.name: lokalise.client.Client.__fetch_attr
documentation:
Searches for the given attribute. Uses populator
to set the attribute if it cannot be found. Used to lazy-load
endpoints.

methods.name: lokalise.client.Client.__clear_endpoint_attrs
documentation: Clears all lazily-loaded endpoint attributes

Client used to send API requests.

Usage:

    import lokalise
    client = lokalise.Client('api_token')
    client.projects()

modules.name: lokalise.oauth_client
documentation:
lokalise.oauth_client
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module contains API client that can be used with OAuth 2 tokens.

classes.name: lokalise.oauth_client.OAuthClient

methods.name: lokalise.oauth_client.OAuthClient.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate a new Lokalise API client with OAuth 2 token.

```
param str token

Your Lokalise API token obtained via OAuth 2 flow.

param connect_timeout

(optional) Server connection timeout
```

(the value is in seconds). By default, the client will wait indefinitely.
\:type connect_timeout: int or float
\:param read_timeout: (optional) Server read timeout
(the value is in seconds). By default, the client will wait indefinitely.
\:type read_timeout: int or float
\:param enable_compression: (optional) Whether to enable gzip compression.
\:param api_host: (optional) Custom API host to send requests to.
By default it's off.
\:type enable_compression: bool
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments,too-many-positional-arguments

Client used to send API requests with OAuth 2 tokens.

Usage:

    import lokalise
    client = lokalise.OAuthClient('oauth2_api_token')
    client.projects()

modules.name: lokalise._version

lokalise.version
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module contains plugin metadata.

modules.name: lokalise.request
documentation:
lokalise.request
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module sends HTTP requests, parses responses, and returns formatted data.

Attributes:

    :attribute str BASE_URL: path to the Lokalise APIv2.
    :attribute list PAGINATION_HEADERS: list of response headers that contain pagination data.

functions.name: lokalise.request.get
documentation:
Performs GET requests

```
param client

Lokalise API client

type client

lokalise.Client

param path

Relative path to the API endpoint

param params

Other request params

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.post
documentation:
Performs POST requests

```
param client

Lokalise API client

type client

lokalise.Client

param path

Relative path to the API endpoint

param params

Other request params

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.put
documentation:
Performs PUT requests

```
param client

Lokalise API client

type client

lokalise.Client

param path

Relative path to the API endpoint

param params

Other request params

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.patch
documentation:
Performs PATCH requests

```
param client

Lokalise API client

type client

lokalise.Client

param path

Relative path to the API endpoint

param params

Other request params

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.delete
documentation:
Performs DELETE requests

```
param client

Lokalise API client

type client

lokalise.Client

param path

Relative path to the API endpoint

param params

Other request params

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.respond_with
documentation:
Converts the response data to JSON and attaches pagination-related data.
An exception will be raised if the response status code is 4xx or 5xx,
or contains an "error" key

```
param response

Response from the API

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.extract_headers_from
documentation:
Fetches pagination-related data from the response headers

```
param response

Response from the API

rtype dict

```

functions.name: lokalise.request.options
documentation:
Prepares proper request options, including Accept headers, API token,
and timeouts.

```
param client

Lokalise API client

type client

lokalise.Client

rtype dict

```

modules.name: lokalise.request_utils
documentation:
lokalise.request_utils
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module provides helpers to send HTTP requests.

functions.name: lokalise.request_utils.raise_on_error
documentation: Raises an error for HTTP codes 400+

functions.name: lokalise.request_utils.respond_with_error
documentation:
Raises an error based on the HTTP status code.
If the status code is unknown, raises a generic ClientError

```
param data

Response body from the API that usually contains error message

param code

Response status code
```

functions.name: lokalise.request_utils.path_to_endpoint
documentation: Prepares the URI to send request to.

functions.name: lokalise.request_utils.__format_params
documentation: Converts request params to JSON

functions.name: lokalise.request_utils.__prepare
documentation: Prepares the URI by stripping all unnecessary slashes

modules.name: lokalise.base_client
documentation:
lokalise.base_client
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
This module contains base API client definition.

classes.name: lokalise.base_client.BaseClient

methods.name: lokalise.base_client.BaseClient.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate a new Lokalise API client.

```
param str token

Your Lokalise API token.

param connect_timeout

(optional) Server connection timeout
```

(the value is in seconds). By default, the client will wait indefinitely.
\:type connect_timeout: int or float
\:param read_timeout: (optional) Server read timeout
(the value is in seconds). By default, the client will wait indefinitely.
\:type read_timeout: int or float
\:param enable_compression: (optional) Whether to enable gzip compression.
\:param api_host: (optional) Custom API host to send requests to.
By default it's off.
\:type enable_compression: bool
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments,too-many-positional-arguments
Base client used to send API requests.

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Collection parent class inherited by specific collections.

classes.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new collection.
To get access to collection data, use `items` attribute.
Pagination-related data is stored inside the following attributes:

> total_count
> page_count
> limit
> current_page

```
param raw_data

Data returned by the API
```

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.is_last_page
documentation:
Checks whether the current collection set is the last page.

```
rtype

bool
```

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.is_first_page
documentation:
Checks whether the current collection set is the first page.

```
rtype

bool
```

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.has_next_page
documentation:
Checks whether the current collection set has the next page.

```
rtype

bool
```

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.has_prev_page
documentation:
Checks whether the current collection set has the previous page.

```
rtype

bool
```

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.has_next_cursor
documentation:
Checks whether the current collection set has the next cursor.

```
rtype

bool
```

methods.name: lokalise.collections.base_collection.BaseCollection.__extract_common_attrs
documentation:
Fetches common data from the response and sets the
corresponding attributes.

Abstract base class for resources collections.

:attribute DATA_KEY: contains the key name that should be used to fetch
collection data. Response usually arrives in the following format:
{"project_id": "abc", contributors: [{"user_id": 1}, {"user_id": 2}]}
In this case, the DATA_KEY would be "contributors"

:attribute MODEL_KLASS: tells which class to use to produce models for each
item in the collection.

:attribute COMMON_ATTRS: list of common attributes that the collections may have.

methods.name: lokalise.oauth2.auth.Auth.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate a new client.

:param str token: Your Lokalise client ID.
:param str token: Your Lokalise client secret.

methods.name: lokalise.oauth2.auth.Auth.auth
documentation:
Generate a new auth URL. Users have to visit it and
explicitly approve the requested permissions

```
param scope

Requested scopes

type scope

list or str

param str redirect_uri

Optional redirect URI

param str state

Optional state to protect from CSRF attacks

rtype str

```

methods.name: lokalise.oauth2.auth.Auth.token
documentation:
Requests a new OAuth 2 token by sending a HTTP POST request.

```
param code

Code obtained with the  method
```

methods.name: lokalise.oauth2.auth.Auth.refresh
documentation:
Refreshes OAuth 2 token by sending a HTTP POST request.

```
param refresh_token

Refresh token obtained with the  method
```

OAuth 2 flow client used to request tokens.

Usage:

    import lokalise
    client = lokalise.Auth('client id', 'client secret')
    url = client.auth()
    client.token('secret code')
    client.refresh('refresh token')

methods.name: lokalise.endpoints.base_endpoint.BaseEndpoint.path_with_params
documentation:
Generates relative path to the endpoint using the template stored
in PATH and the provided ids. Some or all ids may be omitted depending
on the actual endpoint.

Abstract base class for API endpoints. Endpoints are used to load
the actual data.

:attribute PATH: contains the template of the relative URI path to
the API endpoint, for example:

    projects
    projects/system_languages
    projects/$parent_id/contributors
    projects/$parent_id/keys/$resource_id/comments/$subresource_id

The actual path is generated using the provided ids.
